response:
d_emu

This is the most specific exception rule that explains why emma (an emu) doesn't fly, while preserving all other valid inferences. The rule states that emus normally don't fly, which directly addresses the anomaly without affecting other bird properties like having feathers or being warm-blooded.

meta.system:
Pick the single best exception rule. Candidates:
d_emu: emu(X) ~> ~flies(X)
d_emu_broad: bird(X) ~> ~flies(X)
d_emu_wron
meta.user:
Facts: bird(emma), bird(tweety), emu(emma), sparrow(tweety).
General rules (=> means "normally implies"): r_flies: bird(X) => flies(X); r_feathers: bird(X) => has_feathers(X); r_warm: bird(X) => warm_
